info.description:
Backend API for the FitCoach CRM platform.
Authoring format: OpenAPI 2.0 (swaggo). A converted OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec is served at GET /openapi.json.
info.title: FitCoach CRM API

get.description: Redirects the browser to Google's consent screen. Hit this in a browser, not via curl.
Handles Google's redirect after sign-in. On success, 302-redirects to {frontend}/auth/google/callback?access_token=...&refresh_token=...

parameters.description: OAuth state echoed back by Google
parameters.name: state

302.description: Redirect to frontend with access_token and refresh_token query params

400.description: missing code or state, or state mismatch

post.description: Email/password login. Returns access + refresh tokens.
Revokes the supplied refresh token. Idempotent.
Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new access+refresh pair. Old refresh token is revoked. Reuse of a revoked token revokes all of the user's sessions.
Email/password coach registration. Returns access + refresh tokens.

get.description: Coach view, with optional filters. status = "unread" | "responded" | "pending" (read but not yet responded).

patch.description: Saves the coach's private working response. Send {"draft":""} or {"draft":null} to clear.

post.description: Publishes the coach's response. Marks the check-in as read and clears any draft. Use PATCH /draft for autosave.

get.description: Returns the authenticated coach's clients. Supports status filter, free-text search, and offset pagination.

parameters.description: substring match on name or email
parameters.name: search

409.description: email already in use for this coach

delete.description: Sets status to "ended". Historical data (check-ins, sessions, payments) is preserved.

post.description: Generates a one-time portal invite token for a client. The raw token + invite URL is returned in the response so the coach's frontend can send the email. Expires in 7 days. Re-calling supersedes any prior invite.
post.summary: Invite a client to the portal

get.description: Single round-trip via SQL function. Returns active_clients, checkins_this_week, unread_checkins, sessions_today.

patch.description: Records the coach's current onboarding step (1–4). Step can only advance (lower values are ignored). onboarding_done flips to true automatically when step reaches 4.
Updates scalar fields. To replace custom_questions, use PUT /api/v1/me/settings/custom-questions.

put.description: Full replace (PUT semantics). Send the entire desired array. Send an empty array to clear.

get.description: Returns whether the slug is free. The calling coach's own slug always reports available.

post.summary: Mark all my notifications as read (coach)

get.description: Returns the coach's payments newest first. Filter by client, type, status, and created_at range. `to` is exclusive.

parameters.description: paid | pending | failed | overdue | active | cancelling
parameters.name: status

post.description: Only invoices are creatable here. Subscription rows come from Stripe webhooks (later phase). invoice_number is server-generated.

404.description: client_id not owned by this coach

patch.description: Allowed fields: status, description, sent_at, due_date, paid_at. amount_cents and payment_type are immutable.

post.description: Sets status='paid' and paid_at=NOW() atomically. Idempotent on already-paid rows (paid_at gets refreshed; harmless).
Sets the client's portal password using a one-time invite token. Token is consumed on success.

400.description: invalid token, expired, or weak password

post.description: Email/password login for clients. Returns access + refresh tokens.
post.summary: Log in to the client portal
Log out of the client portal

post.description: Client portal: create a new weekly check-in. Returns 409 if a check-in already exists for this week (any date in the week resolves to the same Monday).

409.description: check-in for this week already exists

patch.description: Client self-service partial update. Currently only phone is editable; goal, plan, and program fields are coach-managed.

post.summary: Mark all my notifications as read (client)

get.description: Requires a fresh ticket from POST /api/v1/portal/ws/ticket.

post.description: Returns a single-use, 30-second ticket. Open the WS at /api/v1/portal/ws?ticket={ticket} before expires_at.

get.description: Returns the coach's sessions sorted by start time ascending. Filter by client, type, and date range. `to` is exclusive.

404.description: client_id not owned by this coach

delete.description: Hard delete. For "soft cancel" semantics, PATCH the session with confirmed=false instead.

post.description: Receives and processes Stripe payment events. Signature verified via Stripe-Signature header.

get.description: Requires a fresh ticket from POST /api/v1/ws/ticket. Server pushes JSON-encoded notification payloads — same shape as GET /api/v1/notifications items.
get.summary: Open a coach WebSocket (push channel for notifications)

post.description: Returns a single-use, 30-second ticket. Open the WS at /api/v1/ws?ticket={ticket} before expires_at.

get.description: Returns 200 as long as the process is running. Does not check downstream dependencies.
Pings the database. Returns 503 if the DB is unreachable so load balancers stop routing here.

currency.description:
Currency: any 3-char string. We normalize to lowercase server-side
(see below), so a `lowercase` validator tag here would reject the
input before normalization could lift it.

invite_token.description: raw; coach's frontend emails this to the client

invite_url.description: ready-to-use deep link to the accept-invite page

total.description: total rows matching filters, ignoring pagination

data.description:
`swaggertype:"object"` tells swag to document this as an opaque JSON
object. The actual Go type is json.RawMessage so the embedded event
payload passes through verbatim — the schema is event-type-specific
and intentionally polymorphic at the wire level.
